rationale_overall: Stand at 2022-09-30 close. Macro regime since the last rebalance (2022-06-30): Fed delivered 75bp on 2022-07-27 and another 75bp on 2022-09-21, NASDAQ and S&P down sharply through Q3, UK gilt crisis 2022-09-23 added cross-asset stress, and Nord Stream 1/2 pipelines were sabotaged on 2022-09-26 — a clean reinforcement of the geopolitical-energy thesis. Each of the eight current slots maps to a named profile wave and each has at least one fresh pre-date catalyst supporting hold: (1) MSFT and (2) GOOGL — pre-LLM-mania AI infrastructure / search core, Q3 drawdown is buildup-phase pullback (NASDAQ-wide multiple compression from Fed hikes), not a wave crest; the AI capex thesis is intact and hyperscaler capital intensity is rising. (3) NVDA — the August 2022 Commerce Department A100/H100 China license requirement is a real China-revenue hit, but the CHIPS Act (signed 2022-08-09, $52.7B in chip subsidies + 25% investment tax credit) directly underwrites the US data-center and fab buildout that is NVDA's structural tailwind; net pre-date thesis remains buildup. (4) ISRG — sole robotics name, no negative catalyst pre-date. (5) URA — uranium spot fell roughly 8.7% in September (largest monthly drop since March 2019) and miners lagged, but Japan PM Kishida's 2022-08-24 announcement to restart seven reactors by next summer + explore next-gen builds is a multi-year demand catalyst, and Nord Stream sabotage 2022-09-26 underscores European energy-security pivot toward nuclear; this is a pullback inside a buildup, not a crest. (6) RKLB — Investor Day 2022-09-21 in NYC delivered a Neutron development update (revised to 9-engine first stage on 2022-09-22) and an Electron en-route to Wallops for the first US-soil launch (HawkEye 360); execution catalysts within the quarter. (7) IONQ — sole quantum name in the basket, no pre-date thesis break. (8) LLY — added last quarter on Mounjaro FDA approval (2022-05-13), no pre-date reason to remove and the multi-decade demographics thesis is unchanged. Considered and rejected: re-adding LNG (Cheniere) on the Nord Stream catalyst — URA already holds the geopolitical-energy slot, LNG was removed only one quarter ago on a trim-into-strength rationale, and adding it back now would be whipsaw without a new structural divergence (US LNG export capacity is supply-constrained on a multi-year horizon, not a one-quarter trade). Considered and rejected: LMT/RTX defense primes on Ukraine weapons-replenishment tailwinds — profile names rockets/robotics/quantum/nuclear as next waves, not defense primes; RKLB is the rocket exposure. Considered and rejected: trimming NVDA on the China export-control hit — the CHIPS Act and US data-center demand are the larger pre-date forces; this is buildup-phase noise. Post-decision basket remains AT cap=8 and unchanged: MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, ISRG, URA, RKLB, IONQ, LLY.
